articles.title: Australian ski resorts smash July climate records with temperatures 12C above average
articles.description: Resorts in NSW and Victoria face a warm and dry start to the season with only sporadic falls of natural snow in many alpine areas Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast Alpine ski resorts in New South Wales and Victoria saw climate records shattered at the weekend with maximum temperatures up to 12C above average. articles.title: Australia has had housing crises before: what can we learn from them? | Anne O’Brien
articles.description: When governments make access to housing more equitable, it’s because they recognise its social and economic value The federal government’s first major tax reform in nearly 30 years was a significant achievement that suggests we may have turned a corner on the housing crisis. It is a good time to revisit Australia’s housing history within the broader history of social policy. This is a story of public investment for economic growth as well as for the needs of citizens. articles.title: Moscow targeted by drones while Russia bombards Kyiv
articles.description: Ukraine launches its latest attack on the Russian capital, firing more than 400 drones, while the Kremlin deploys more ballistic missiles against targets across Ukraine.

articles.title: Joyce defends Hanson's controversial meeting as dog interrupts interview

articles.title: Antibiotic-resistant bacteria increasingly leaving children vulnerable to common infections, global study finds
articles.description: Exclusive: Research spanning 82 countries finds antibiotic resistance increased in every region between 2004 and 2022 Harry Booth has had a rough run through Australia’s coldest months. The 13-year-old has been in and out of hospital with a range of common chest and urinary tract infections that his doctors find challenging to treat.
